title: Koanophyllon droserolepis
taxonomy: Koanophyllon droserolepis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Brazil and is often classified as an endemic species. The plant's classification is further divided into the genus Koanophyllon and the specific epithet droserolepis. Koanophyllon droserolepis is a member of the tribe Heliantheae, within the subfamily Asteroideae. Further research into the plant's classification may provide additional information about its taxonomic relationships.

intro: Koanophyllon droserolepis, also known as the "Mexican Buttercup", is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, characterized by its yellow flowers and unique leaf structure. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Bioactive constituents of Koanophyllon droserolepis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
